notes: Template educational seeds mapped to AAL labels.

stimuli.label: Hearing music
stimuli.explanation: Auditory cortices are commonly involved in early processing of musical input. This simplified model emphasizes bilateral Heschl and superior temporal seeds before broader network spread.

stimuli.label: Pinprick / acute pain
stimuli.explanation: Primary somatosensory and salience-related regions are commonly involved in nociceptive tasks. This template shows a conservative educational pattern, not a clinical pain metric.

stimuli.label: Fear / threat
stimuli.explanation: Amygdala and salience regions are often associated with threat processing. This is a simplified educational map and does not imply deterministic emotional circuitry.

stimuli.label: Reward / surprise
stimuli.explanation: Medial orbitofrontal and related valuation regions are commonly involved in reward processing paradigms. This educational template emphasizes broad network-level association only.
